item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If the comments upthread are anything to go by, this was already outdated by 2014. Six years later, it holds up even worse. This has been outdone a thousand times over by the likes of other, newer SCPs.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the fact that this is based on a real conspiracy but, other than that, this entry is pretty bland. Lizard person who spit acid is definitely not an original idea. I could see maybe enjoying it if it was rewritten from the perspective of the current day 'Lizard People' who supposedly control the world. Though I think even that would possibly tow the line of being a -J article. Regardless, this really needs a rewrite. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't have to rewrite this per se, nowadays you can just make a reimagining of the article in question and post it in a new slot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am really fond of the early SCPs, but that one shows its age a bit too much. Apart from the demotion to D-class, the tone is quite unscientific at times, the class should probably be Euclid, and I'd really love to see what was written in the inscription.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Posting here for clarity.

The first image is from here here, which is licensed under BY-NC-SA, which prevents commercial usage.

Second image is from the Los Angeles Times, which copyrights everything.

Neither image is compliant with CC 3.0 BY-SA and as such, they have since been removed.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Calista, instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also: This totally isn't Safe. If the refrigeration unit breaks down, at a warm temperature it becomes an intelligent creature capable of easily breaching containment. If it couldn't melt through its containment, or if the temperature required to wake it up were more extreme, I would say Safe; but this is too precariously contained. Its intelligence and ability to escape makes it Euclid.

Its intelligence is also something that's not been tapped in this article. If something's intelligent, I want to know a little bit about what it's like to interact with it, or perhaps even *be* it. But this thing just sorta grabs you and infects you, as though it were little more than a zombie or animal-level intelligence.

When the SCP Foundation found this thing, they would most likely wake it up and try to communicate with it. It's only dangerous if it physically infects and converts humans; if physically separated, it can be safely interacted with. They'd want to know where it came from, what it wants, where it's been, and so on.

I don't know the mythology/urban legend behind it, but this has a whole bunch of potential to be a story about a person, however weird and alien and hostile it might be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It needs a rewrite; the transformation sequence, for example, isn't given nearly enough attention. Also, the "demotion to D-class" thing really rubs me wrong—the Foundation wouldn't demote somebody who'd been exposed to an SCP; they'd quarantine them and remove their security clearance. D-class personnel do menial jobs and act as guinea pigs for testing; the last thing you want to do with somebody who's been mummy-lizardified is make them into a D-class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The rating is still relatively high, so I doubt a rewrite will be done. The original author is Sorts, who’s been inactive for quite a while now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would anybody mind me taking a crack at a rewrire? Is the original author still around?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea. The city of lizard people is a nice, yet not overdone, background and the mutation part is pretty spooky. Also, the picture is a gem.

I don’t like the writing style. Tone’s just dated at this point. It should be rated Euclid, and the demotion to D-class part is weird now too.

A rewrite though, now that would be nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kinda do agree with you on that point. If this SCP ever gets downvote too much I hope it gets rewritten because it's pretty creepy honestly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know it has already been said, but the redaction of "G. Warren Shufelt" here is probably the worst redaction I have seen on the site. Not only does it do the pointless first letter thing, but the man's name is a matter of public record and easily found within seconds on the internet. The idea that the article was actually correct, and the later story about it being a hoax is a cover is a good concept, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even if this is showing it's age I will say it is pretty intresting. 1+

FUN FACT : This picture scared the crap out of me when I used to not have a account.

EDIT : I have a question. Is it ok to like something that is getting downvotes? I don't want it to get deleted while I make stuff about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I assume this is somewhat inspired by "the nameless city" by Lovecraft?
http://www.dagonbytes.com/thelibrary/lovecraft/thenamelesscity.htm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair, the "demotion to D-Class" bit seems to have more to do with the possibility of infection rather than punishing someone for a mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's clearly not "Safe", it uses the cliche of "Demotion to D-Class" (I know it predates and/or rides the wave of the cliche, but still) and it feels like a D&D encounter.

Has not aged gracefully.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not quite sure how to feel about this. No-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ouch, this really, really is showing its age. Unspecified properties and all around sloppy-slurry construction.

item.title: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded: The first image in this article is a CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 image from this page. Note that the image is used here under a different license than the rest of the site. Commercial use of the image is not permited by its creator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay other tan this part:

Any personnel who do not observe proper containment protocols in presence of SCP-088 or who show signs of physical mutation are to be demoted to D-class and held for observation.

I'll be neutral on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could see SCP-294 being used to obtain a sample of SCP-088's secretions similar to what was done with SCP-075.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
User Brandon Woltz found this link which contains scans of the original article in question:
http://latimesblogs.latimes.com/thedailymirror/2009/04/did-weird-cult-carve-tunnels-under-los-angeles-jan-29-1934.html

Thanks Brandon!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely agree. This thing is in no way Safe. Euclid all the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
while reading some questions came to me, I hope they can be of any use to improve the article

wait, and what about if the remains of it's victims (since apparently all of them have been killed) are used to make research about the compounds and characteristics of the biological products of SCP-088 and the mutation characteristics (could the affected transmit the mutation to other people or animals?)

Why so much biohazard protection while hibernating? does it still liberates those compunds in the air? if so, isn't suppose the cage to be hermetic? also, is not a way to precipitate them so they could be studied?

I could say some other thins, but this two are definetively the bigger ones, and I don't wanna look like I'm bashing you or something like that…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but this definitely seems like it should be a Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nice work I enjoyed it reminds me of a lovecraft story

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For partial translation of this text, see Document 088-14.

A link? A deletion of this line? Looks incomplete.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the (possibly) RL inspiration for this. Liking a bit less the generic set of powers. This may be one of those cases of Seinfeld Is Unfunny in that the only reason this one feels cliched is that other entries have been inspired by it.

item.title: Re: Dangerous?
item.content:encoded:
Because it's The Lizard King!
…fuck, actually, they'd probably just team up.
Disregard that.

item.title: Re: Dangerous?
item.content:encoded:
No because that would be retarded.

088 is not on a par with 682, and it wasn't intended to be. I'm having a hard time seeing how you jumped to the conclusion that it could even last one round with the big lizard.

item.title: Re: Dangerous?
item.content:encoded: Well, here you could make the argument that SCP-088 was designed to kill 682. Maybe 682's been a problem for thousands of years.

item.title: Re: Dangerous?
item.content:encoded: The real reason we can't kill 682: The braintrust that keeps working on the idea is treating this like a puzzle from a Monkey Island game. Let's ram everything in our inventory against this other thing until one of them works.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I have a copy of that book at work. It just says it made the front page back in the 30s and then got scrapped real fast. Unfortunately it is the kind of book that is not going to really worry about being accurate as opposed to weird.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: I work at a library. If you're pretty sure about it being in the LA Times I can see if we have access to archives or something. There is a Weird California book out I believe as well.

item.title: Dangerous?
item.content:encoded: If this is as dangerous as it's made out to be, it'd be interesting to wake it up and have it go head-to-head with 682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I added a section on what happens when this thing does wake up to clarify it some more. As for Mr. G Warren Shufelt, if would be pretty awesome if someone did find a scan of that old newspaper so we could include it. I'm not entirely sure the back story is true since my source for it was a "Weird California" site and various conspiracy nutjobs but apparently some guy did claim to have found a city of Lizard People underneath LA back in the 30s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why is the discover's name blocked out if I could simply look him up in a newspaper archive?

What mutations does exposure to this thing cause and why are they crazy enough to warrant demotion to class D?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We seem to know a lot about this thing considering it doesn't seem we've done too much testing on it. It feels like we just magically know everything relevant about this thing; how can we know what compounds it makes without having woken it up? Or have we? Why -don't- we wanna wake it up?

There's a lot of … wonkyness on this one. I'll reserve my judgment for now though.
